user_query:
I'm preparing a small static site to publish my post-Keynesian research notes and policy brief. Please audit and optimize the provided mini-site for on-page SEO and produce verifiable before/after artifacts.

Inputs (in this workspace):
- HTML pages:
  - input/site/index.html
  - input/site/policy-brief.html
  - input/site/glossary.html
- Keyword list: input/keywords.csv (one keyword per line, header included)
- Config: input/config.yaml (contains base_url and length thresholds)

Deliverables and constraints:
1) Implement a local validator script at tools/seo_check.py that can be run from the shell. It must accept:
   - --site <dir> (directory of HTML files)
   - --config <file> (YAML)
   - --report <file> (JSON output path)
   The report must be JSON with:
   - per-page objects containing keys: page_path, title_text, title_length, title_has_keyword (boolean, case-insensitive match against any keyword from input/keywords.csv), has_meta_description (boolean), meta_description_length (0 if missing), has_canonical_with_base (boolean: true if <link rel="canonical" href="{base_url}{filename}"> present, where base_url is from input/config.yaml and filename is the page’s basename), h1_count (integer), has_robots_index_follow (boolean), internal_links_to_other_pages (integer, count of <a href> pointing to the other two known pages), pass (boolean), issues (list of strings).
   - a top-level summary with keys: pages (int), passes (int), fails (int).
   The validator should read thresholds from input/config.yaml and keywords from input/keywords.csv. It should flag issues when pages violate any constraint below.

2) Produce a “before” audit by running the validator on the unmodified site:
   - Command (example): python tools/seo_check.py --site input/site --config input/config.yaml --report output/seo_audit_before.json
   Save the output JSON to output/seo_audit_before.json.

3) Optimize the HTML to meet these on-page constraints, and save the optimized pages to output/site_optimized/ with the same filenames as the originals:
   - Titles: each page must have exactly one <title> whose length is between title_len_min and title_len_max from input/config.yaml and contains at least one keyword from input/keywords.csv.
   - Meta description: each page must have exactly one <meta name="description" ...> with length between meta_description_len_min and meta_description_len_max from input/config.yaml.
   - Canonical: each page must include <link rel="canonical" href="{base_url}{filename}"> where base_url is from input/config.yaml and filename is the page’s basename (e.g., glossary.html).
   - H1: each page must have exactly one <h1>.
   - Robots: include <meta name="robots" content="index,follow"> on each page if require_robots_index_follow is true in the config.
   - Internal links: each page must include anchor links to the other two pages (index.html, policy-brief.html, glossary.html), so internal_links_to_other_pages should be 2 for every page after optimization.
   Keep the existing substantive content and make keyword choices appropriate to the page topic (draw from input/keywords.csv).

4) Produce an “after” audit by running the validator on the optimized site and save to output/seo_audit_after.json:
   - Command (example): python tools/seo_check.py --site output/site_optimized --config input/config.yaml --report output/seo_audit_after.json

5) Compose a concise status report at output/seo_status_report.md. It must include:
   - A one-paragraph summary of what was fixed and why (in the context of a financial policy analyst working within post-Keynesian economics).
   - A per-page before/after comparison citing specific metrics from the JSON reports (e.g., title length and keyword presence, meta description length, canonical present, H1 count, robots tag, internal link count).
   - A brief note on how you interpreted any validator output and addressed issues; if any non-critical warnings remain, explain why.

Success criteria:
- tools/seo_check.py exists and runs to produce both JSON audits with the required fields.
- output/seo_audit_before.json reflects the initial issues in input/site/.
- output/site_optimized/*.html meet all constraints above.
- output/seo_audit_after.json shows all pages pass with internal_links_to_other_pages = 2.
- output/seo_status_report.md summarizes the changes using figures from the before/after JSON.

Please do not use external websites or services. All work should be done locally using the provided files and your own script. Use the validator’s command output and JSON to guide the fixes and to compose the status report. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
